theory.canonical_statement: This generalizes Einstein's field equations by including gauge field strengths and quantum vacuum contributions; it states that geometry responds to the sum of all forms of energy and vacuum fluctuations.
theory.technical_interpretation: This generalizes Einstein's field equations by including gauge field strengths and quantum vacuum contributions; it states that geometry responds to the sum of all forms of energy and vacuum fluctuations.

latex_blocks:
T_{\mu\nu}^{\mathrm{tot}}
=
T_{\mu\nu}^{\mathrm{grav}}+
T_{\mu\nu}^{\mathrm{EM}}+
T_{\mu\nu}^{\mathrm{weak}}+
T_{\mu\nu}^{\mathrm{strong}}+
T_{\mu\nu}^{\mathrm{vac}}
canonical_equation.source_section_latex:
\[
G_{\mu\nu}+\Lambda g_{\mu\nu}
=
\frac{8\pi G}{c^4}\,T_{\mu\nu}^{\mathrm{tot}}
\]
\[
T_{\mu\nu}^{\mathrm{tot}}
=
T_{\mu\nu}^{\mathrm{grav}}+
T_{\mu\nu}^{\mathrm{EM}}+
T_{\mu\nu}^{\mathrm{weak}}+
T_{\mu\nu}^{\mathrm{strong}}+
T_{\mu\nu}^{\mathrm{vac}}
\]
\[
\nabla^{\nu}T_{\mu\nu}^{\mathrm{tot}}=0
\]

equation_progression.interpretive_role: This states the overall field-equation balance.
